svg.text: Framework stack — one tool per job

g.text: Graph-based state machine — handles cycles (QA retry loop),
Claude Sonnet 4 — naturalness injector + SSML annotator
Claude Haiku 4.5 — fact-checker (fast, cheap per-claim checks)
Claude Sonnet 4 — QA reviewer (full script scan)
Conditional edge: QA fail → re-route to dialogue engine (max 2 retries)
Primary: ElevenLabs — best conversational quality, emotion control
Budget: Fish Audio / Kokoro — open-source, near-EL quality at 80% less
Fallback: Google Cloud TTS Neural2 — full SSML, reliable
Per-utterance WAV output — parallel TTS calls across chapters
pydub + ffmpeg — overlap engine, crossfades, mixing
pydub overlay() — backchannel mixing, ambient room tone
Async endpoint, webhook for completion, S3 upload
ECS Fargate — long-running pipeline container (~5-10 min per podcast)
S3 — source chunks, scripts, WAV files, final MP3
Bedrock — Claude API access (or direct Anthropic API)

svg.text: LangGraph over CrewAI — need cycles (QA retry), conditional routing, state persistence
ElevenLabs over PlayHT — PlayHT shut down Dec 2025; EL has best podcast voices
pydub over raw ffmpeg — pythonic API for overlay/crossfade, ffmpeg as backend
Podcastfy (open-source) — study for reference, but build custom for naturalness control
